title: Vir Biotechnology and GSK announce VIR-7831 reduces hospitalisation and risk of death in early treatment of adults with COVID-19
text: Vir Biotechnology and GSK announced positive efficacy results from the Phase 3 COMET-ICE study evaluating VIR-7831, a fully human monoclonal antibody, for early treatment of adults with mild to moderate COVID-19. The COMET-ICE study assessed the safety and efficacy of a single intravenous infusion of VIR-7831 or placebo in approximately 1,300 non-hospitalized participants globally. The primary efficacy endpoint was the proportion of patients who have progression of COVID-19 as defined by the need for hospitalization or death within 29 days of randomization. VIR-7831 showed the ability to neutralize SARS-CoV-2 live virus in vitro and in vivo. It is a fully human anti-SARS-CoV-2 monoclonal antibody selected based on its potential to neutralize the virus, kill infected cells, provide a high barrier to resistance, and achieve high concentrations in the lungs. The Phase 3 portion of the COMET-ICE study expansion included treatment of hospitalized patients and another study for the prevention of symptomatic infection. VIR-7831 has the potential to be used as both a therapeutic and prophylactic treatment.
